findings.title: First-timer meets 'woodshed' and mastery counts without a plain-language onboarding path
findings.detail: Interactive activation promises exemplar counts and Gamma defaults but assumes the user already knows what woodshed means, what an exemplar L-level is, and why mastery order matters. A novice opening Gary directly may feel they must choose correctly among capabilities with no guided 'start here' story.
findings.action: Add a two-sentence plain-English primer in On Activation or a linked micro-reference: what woodshed does, recommended first step (L1-L2), and that production work usually arrives via Marcus.

findings.title: Context envelope fields are described in prose but not as a machine-checkable contract
findings.detail: Marcus delegation lists production run ID, content type, objectives, constraints, style sections, and exemplar refs — but there is no canonical schema, required vs optional fields, or example envelope. Automators and Marcus implementations may diverge silently; Gary's 'missing context' path is correct but discovery of gaps happens late.
findings.action: Add `references/context-envelope-schema.md` (or a YAML example) with required keys, optional keys, and one full golden envelope for headless and orchestrator tests.

findings.title: Structured return to Marcus lacks a versioned output contract for pipelines
findings.detail: Outbound bullets name artifact path, scores, parameter decisions, and flags, but chained agents and CI cannot rely on a single parseable shape. The quality-assessment reference shows YAML — production return could drift between sessions or implementers.
findings.action: Define a stable `gary_return` YAML/JSON schema (version field, paths relative to project root, explicit enums for recommendation) and reference it from SKILL.md Outbound section.

findings.title: Templates assume medical physician audience; non-clinical courses still map through medical framing
findings.detail: When Marcus passes a non-medical content type or audience, Gary is instructed to pick the closest template — tone and audience strings remain medically scoped, which can skew Gamma's text generation until manually overridden.
findings.action: Add a short 'audience override' note: when envelope.audience differs from template, replace tone/audience in the merged YAML before call; optionally add one non-medical template stub.

findings.title: Gamma API pre-flight failure leaves the first-run script underspecified
findings.detail: First interaction requires confirming Gamma connectivity via GammaClient or themes list, but there is no prescribed user-visible fallback if credentials are missing, .env is denied, or the client errors. The user may hit a dead end after sidecar setup.
findings.action: Document a graceful branch: report exact failure class (auth, network, missing dependency), point to gamma-api-mastery setup, and offer 'defer API check; study exemplar briefs only' if appropriate.

findings.title: Write-back depends on production-coordination skill script that may be absent in some workspaces
findings.detail: Learned preferences route through `manage_style_guide.py` in another skill. In a minimal checkout, Gary can read defaults but silently cannot persist learning, creating success amnesia across sessions without an obvious in-skill warning.
findings.action: In SG reference, add a detect-and-report step: if script path missing, log to Marcus return flags and append a one-line user-facing note to use manual style guide edit or install coordination skill.

findings.title: Doc refresh is mandatory before woodshed but has no soft gate or time-box
findings.detail: Users jumping into reproduction may wait on changelog + Ref MCP scans every time. There is no 'use last refresh if less than N days' or 'anything else before I scan the API?' style elicitation — which can feel like ceremony for experts or stall creative flow.
findings.action: Add optional fast path: if `last_refreshed` within policy window and user or envelope says `skip_doc_refresh: true`, skip to exemplar analysis with an explicit risk flag in the return payload.

findings.title: Quality assessment is single-lens self-scoring with no parallel review pattern
findings.detail: Gary scores dimensions honestly, but high-stakes slides might benefit from a second lens (skeptic on embellishment, accessibility spotter) before Marcus approves. The skill does not suggest fan-out or cross-check prompts.
findings.action: Optional finalization step: 'parallel review' mode — second pass with rubric-only adversarial prompt or delegation hint to a QA skill when overall_score < threshold or embellishment_detected.

findings.title: Session end and 'meaningful learning' are subjective for when to save
findings.detail: Save triggers include 'session end if meaningful learning' without cues for compaction-heavy chats or user abort mid-woodshed. Risk of lost transient index context or over-writing ad-hoc sections without user confirmation.
findings.action: Clarify: on explicit user 'stop' or new delegation, flush transient ad-hoc section; offer one-line confirm before large patterns.md append after failed runs.

findings.title: Power users with a complete envelope still share the same activation narrative as explorers
findings.detail: Experts who already have YAML parameters, a frozen style guide, and a content type want to skip greeting and mastery small talk. Today both modes are described but there is no crisp 'expert one-shot' trigger phrase or envelope flag to jump straight to invoke gamma-api-mastery + QA return.
findings.action: Document `expert_fast_path` or equivalent: if envelope contains `parameters_ready: true` and full Gamma parameter block, skip interactive greet and woodshed prompts; execute merge order and return.

findings.title: User who invoked Gary by mistake has no scripted wrong-agent recovery
findings.detail: Confused users asking for PRD or brainstorming get a specialist tuned for Marcus and Gamma. There is no single-line redirect to bmad-help or Marcus beyond Gary staying in persona.
findings.action: Add a capability row or activation bullet: if request clearly outside slides/Gamma, respond once with correct routing suggestion and stop.

findings.title: Ad-hoc mode read-only sidecar is correct but invisible to the delegating user
findings.detail: Marcus or the user may not realize experimental runs do not update patterns — leading to false expectations that Gary 'learned' from a scratch generation.
findings.action: Require Gary to state mode in structured return: `memory_mode: default|ad-hoc` and whether patterns were written.

findings.title: Strength: degradation handling is explicit and actionable across API, quality, circuit breaker, and missing context
findings.detail: Failure modes tie to concrete next steps (retry timing, stronger instructions, Canva fallback, structured failure-report). This reduces dead-end frustration compared to agents that only report errors.

findings.title: Strength: capability table with codes and reference routes supports progressive disclosure well
findings.detail: PR/SG/QA/ES/CT/SM map cleanly to files without loading everything at once — good for token efficiency and for models that load references on demand.

skill_understanding.purpose: Act as Gary, a Gamma API slide specialist for medical education: merge style guide and envelopes, recommend parameters, invoke gamma-api-mastery, assess quality, persist learning to a memory sidecar, and support woodshed exemplar mastery.
skill_understanding.primary_user: Marcus orchestrator (primary production path); course authors and power users for direct woodshed, debugging, and exemplar study.
assumptions: Marcus supplies a sufficiently complete context envelope when delegating.
style_guide.yaml and course_context.yaml exist at expected paths when needed.
Audience is physician-oriented unless envelope overrides; templates are medically framed.
gamma-api-mastery and optionally woodshed and production-coordination tooling are available.
English/default BMad language config unless config overrides.

user_journeys.summary: Opens Gary, sees mastery counts and woodshed — may not know execution order or that Marcus normally delegates; may wander into references without a single happy path.
friction_points: Choosing between interactive vs headless description
bright_spots: Clear Does Not Do and Principles

user_journeys.summary: Has parameters and content ready; wants one-shot generation and terse return — may find greeting and exemplar status noise.
friction_points: Doc refresh before every woodshed cycle
bright_spots: Merge order documented in SG and PR

user_journeys.summary: Invoked Gary for non-slide work; Gary stays in specialist persona.
friction_points: No explicit handoff line to general help or PM agents
bright_spots: Narrow scope reduces misleading answers if user corrects intent

user_journeys.summary: Valid but odd input — new content type, contradictory style overrides, or preserve mode with very long input.
friction_points: Closest-template heuristic may miss pedagogical intent
bright_spots: CT capability documents extension to patterns.md

user_journeys.summary: Missing files, API down, Ref MCP unavailable for doc refresh, or write-back script missing.
friction_points: Silent inability to persist style learning if script missing
bright_spots: Degradation section covers 429 and quality failures

user_journeys.summary: CI or subagent passes envelope and expects deterministic artifact path and parseable status.
friction_points: Human gates implied for woodshed and approval write-back
bright_spots: Headless delegation path described in On Activation
YAML examples for parameters and quality_assessment

autonomous_assessment.suggested_output_contract: Parseable object (YAML or JSON) with keys: schema_version, production_run_id, artifact_paths[], quality_assessment (dimensions + embellishment flags), parameter_decisions, recommendations[], memory_mode, errors[]. Optional markdown summary for human inbox.
required_inputs: learning_objectives or explicit waiver flag from Marcus
style_guide path readable or embedded overrides
autonomous_assessment.notes: Production delegation from Marcus is largely automatable when the envelope is complete. Woodshed, doc refresh policy, user approval for style write-back, and ambiguous objectives remain fundamentally HITL. An explicit headless/expert flag would unlock most friction for pipelines without diluting interactive mastery flows.

top_insights.title: Version the context envelope and Gary return payload
top_insights.detail: Orchestration and CI reliability jump when Marcus and Gary agree on required keys and a single parseable return shape.
top_insights.action: Add small schema docs plus one golden example for each direction; reference them from SKILL.md.

top_insights.title: Expert fast-path is the highest-leverage delight win
top_insights.detail: Reduces perceived latency and token cost for repeat users who already mastered parameters.
top_insights.action: Document envelope flag and behavior: skip greet, run merge + API + QA, return.

top_insights.title: Optional second-lens QA before handoff
top_insights.detail: Protects publication quality when self-scores are borderline or embellishment is detected.
top_insights.action: Gate a cheap adversarial rubric pass on threshold triggers rather than always-on cost.

summary.assessment: Gary is unusually strong on failure handling, scope discipline, and progressive disclosure. The biggest creative wins are formalizing Marcus↔Gary contracts for automation, adding an expert one-shot path, and softening first-run and doc-refresh friction without weakening API safety. Woodshed remains rightly interactive; production delegation can become pipeline-grade with minimal persona change.
